plan_metadata.name: Feature Simple Bot ts Local Debug With Different Account

steps.description: Click the "Teams Agents and Apps" option in the "New Project" dropdown menu within the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit interface.
Click the "General Teams Agent" option in the dropdown menu within the "Teams Agent or App Using Teams AI Library" dialog.
Click the "Simple Bot" option in the "Teams Capability" dropdown menu.
Click the "TypeScript" option in the "Programming Language" dropdown menu within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Default folder" field in the "Workspace Folder" dialog to select the path "/home/vscode/AgentsToolkitProjects".
Click the "Close" (X) button at the top-right corner of the Microsoft Teams web interface in the Chrome browser to close the current tab.

Activate the Visual Studio Code “Command Palette” by pressing Ctrl+Shift+P in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit workspace, with accounts and environment panels visible on the left and terminal output displayed below.
Type the text 'accounts' into the Visual Studio Code Command Palette input box at the top center of the interface.
Click the "Microsoft 365 Agents: Accounts" entry in the VS Code command palette to select the related command for account management.
Click the "Sign out of Microsoft 365: ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}}" option in the account dropdown menu at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Sign out" button on the sign-out confirmation dialog for the account '${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}}' within the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Sign in to Microsoft 365" account entry in the ACCOUNTS panel of the MICROSOFT 365 AGENTS TOOLKIT extension within Visual Studio Code to initiate or check Microsoft 365 sign-in status.
Click the "Sign in" button in the Microsoft 365 developer sandbox prompt at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Use another account" option on the Microsoft "Pick an account" login screen in Google Chrome.
Type the email address '${{env:MS_AZURE_ACCOUNT_NAME}}' into the Microsoft login page's email or username input field within the Chrome browser.
Click the "Next" button on the Microsoft sign-in form after entering the email address in the Chrome browser login.microsoftonline.com interface.
Type '${{secret:MS_AZURE_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}}' into the password field on the Microsoft login page for the user in the Google Chrome browser.
Click the blue "Sign in" button on the Microsoft login page in Google Chrome after entering the account password.
Click the "Close" (X) button in the top-right corner of the Google Chrome window after successful Visual Studio Code sign-in, as indicated by the red crosshair.

Use keyboard shortcut Ctrl+Shift+P to open the Command Palette in Visual Studio Code with the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit extension active. This enables command search or execution during agent/app development and troubleshooting, as indicated by the toolkit sidebar and error output panel.
Type the text 'select and start debug' into the Visual Studio Code command palette input field at the top of the interface, triggering the "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" option.
Click the "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" option in the Visual Studio Code command palette, located at the top-center of the Microsoft 365 Agents extension interface.

Click the "Debug in Teams (Chrome)" option in the Visual Studio Code command palette, immediately below the search input field containing "debug", to initiate debugging in Teams (Chrome) within the Microsoft 365 Agents Playground workspace.

Click the "Cancel" button in the Microsoft 365 account environment mismatch dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface. The red crosshair precisely marks the "Cancel" text, ensuring the dialog is dismissed.

Press **Ctrl+Shift+P** in Visual Studio Code with the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit open to activate the command palette, enabling access to commands related to agent/app creation, debugging, and environment configuration.
Type text: 'select and start debugging' into the VS Code command palette input field at the top of the Microsoft 365 Agents extension interface.

Click the "Debug: Select and Start Debugging" option in the Visual Studio Code command palette, located in the Microsoft 365 Agents Playground extension interface. The interaction targets the highlighted command entry, visually marked by a blue background and bold text.
Click the "Debug in Teams (Chrome)" option in the command palette dropdown within Visual Studio Code, directly under the search box containing the term "debug".

Click the "Continue" button in the Microsoft 365 account mismatch warning dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface. The dialog prompts the user to sign out and sign in with the correct account.
Click the "Sign out" button in the confirmation dialog for signing out of '${{env:MS_AZURE_ACCOUNT_NAME}}' at the top center of the Visual Studio Code interface.
Click the "Sign in" button in the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit permission dialog at the top of the Visual Studio Code interface to initiate authentication.
Click the ${{env:M365_ACCOUNT_NAME}} account option in the Microsoft sign-in dialog within Google Chrome.
Type '${{secret:M365_ACCOUNT_PASSWORD}}' into the password input field on the Microsoft login page for the account.
Click the "Sign in" button on the Microsoft account login page after entering the password, highlighted by the red crosshair.
Click the "Close" (X) button at the top-right corner of the Google Chrome window displaying the Visual Studio Code sign-in confirmation page.
Click the "Open" button in the app details dialog within the Microsoft Teams web interface.
Click the "Open" button in the "Let's go" dialog of the app within the Microsoft Teams web interface.
Click the "Hi" suggestion card to initiate a conversation with the bot in the Microsoft Teams web app. The interaction point is on the "Hi" card under the bot chat interface.
Click the "Send" button in the Microsoft Teams web app chat interface to submit the message "Hi".
@assertion bot reply message: "[1] you said: Hi" exist.
